texto: As for my own part I care not for death, for all men are mortal; and though I be a woman yet I have as good a courage answerable to my place as ever my father had. I am your anointed Queen. I will never be by violence constrained to do anything. I thank God I am indeed endowed with such qualities that if I were turned out of the realm in my petticoat I were able to live in any place in Christendom.

texto: I grieve and dare not show my discontent, I love and yet am forced to seem to hate,I do, yet dare not say I ever meant, I seem stark mute but inwardly do prate. I am and not, I freeze and yet am burned, Since from myself another self I turned. My care is like my shadow in the sun, Follows me flying, flies when I pursue it, Stands and lies by me, doth what I have done.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Elizabeth I: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I grieve and dare not show my discontent, I love and yet am forced to seem to hate,I do, yet dare…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, elizabeth, legacy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de legacy e men.
